channel.title: International Journal of Nursing Education Scholarship
channel.description: A RSS feed containing the latest articles published in International Journal of Nursing Education Scholarship

item.title: Don’t interrupt me! development of a handoff education bundle to simulate the real world
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0092

item.title: Gerontological educational interventions for student nurses: a systematic review of qualitative findings
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0042

item.title: Educational interventions to improve student nurses’ knowledge, attitudes, or willingness to work with older people: a systematic review of quantitative findings
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0110

item.title: Barriers to and facilitators of accelerated BSN students’ success: a scoping review with possible strategies for success
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2024-0018

item.title: Formal nursing focused academic practice partnerships for advancing nursing research and scholarship: a scoping review protocol
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0058

item.title: Notetaking among nursing students
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2024-0002

item.title: Nursing students’ experiences of teaching and learning during the COVID-19 pandemic: a systematic review and meta-synthesis of qualitative studies
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0094

item.title: Relationship between tutor support, caring self-efficacy and intention to leave of nursing students: the roles of self-compassion as mediator and moderator
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0101

item.title: Knowledge, attitudes, and practices toward infection control precautions among nurses in Palestinian hospitals
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0117

item.title: AI-generated vs. student-crafted assignments and implications for evaluating student work in nursing: an exploratory reflection
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0098

item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2024-0035

item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2024-0003

item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0084

item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0115

item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0064

item.title: Comparison of the traditional and block mode of delivery on undergraduate nursing students’ perceived levels of preparedness to use evidence-based practice: a two-group experimental study
item.description: Article by Reed S. Halterman, Shannon Broxton, Thomas Joshua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijnes-2023-0082

item.title: Simulation design – peer-to-peer training as preparation for full-scale scenarios

item.title: The effects of using a flipped classroom pedagogy in nursing anesthesia education: a program evaluation
